reviews.review_text: Nice park with friendly staff. The surroundings are not that interesting, but youre close to gold coast and good surf spots. Also, its relatively cheap compared to other places in the area. Dont go full speed on the slide in the swimming pool, youll get some nice bruises
Lady on front counter(Linda) was the rudest person I have ever dealt with!  Pizza van was on site but not open the entire weekend we were there.Will never return….

Rock hard ground you can’t even get a peg in, we tried a multiple different pegs and bolts with impact drills.. the staff just laughed at us when we informed them of this, they are horrible and full of self importance! very hot, no shade no vibe not busy at all and I can see why, we cut out stay early as the staff were that bad. Avoid this campsite if you can.
Great little caravan park. Great amenities. Fun waterslide.

Have been quite a few times now but this time we weren't so happy Amenities were not cleaned as frequently as they should have been for the amount of people that were staying there. Being Easter you would think they would have activities for the children and the Easter bunny like most other places we have stayed have. Overall not really impressed this time round

A nice place to stay for a couple of nights, amenities always clean with laundry facilities available☺️ I was a bit worried before arriving after seeing all the reviews about the midges. We came prepared with bushman’s with deet and also mosquito coils and we were barely bit so I think as long as you’re covered up/ have some bug spray  you’ll be fine!

An extreme number of the midges. The facility is good. But don’t recommend coming, because of the midges, unless you don’t sit outside your tent. You can experience how many lumps on your body you had the most in your life.

Tent sites are good. Powered & Unpowered. Caravan sites & Cabin's. Heated Pool, Fitness Equipment, Playground, Games Room with 2x Massage chairs.  Wifi is limited. Showers are good. Alway's nice & clean. Food Truck's come in Friday & Saturday night's.
It's a nice place. With Nice Staff.

The park is kept well maintained. We had a powered site with an ensuite. The ensuite we had was not next to our site and I found it a little difficult navigating between the vans to get there. The pool was lovely and it seemed to be the only way to get relief from the sandflies. I will never go back there as the sandflies were unbearable.
What a fantastic holiday spot ,its the most relaxing place ive ever stated
Massive MIDGES problem at tent sites, after exiting your tent you have to run…
Nice place to stay.

Great park, amenities are second to none and service was great. Come prepared for the midges it is unbelievable how many there are, we came prepared with mozzie coils, bushman’s, aero guard and candles having read previous reviews. We were still eaten alive.
This park is the best down this way!! It was so nice to be greeted by the lovely Kathryn or Kathleen . Keep up the good work!!!;
Really nice cabins great view

Staying for 4 weeks on a powered site.
Easy access onto site and plenty of room even with our 22ft van  and  a new Ford Ranger. Amenities are super clean.
A short 2 min drive to supermarkets and shopping centre and 3 min to Coolangatta. Highly recommend.

We have stayed at various different caravan parks throughout our travels and this is by far the best park that we have stayed at. Beautifully laid out park, great amenities and attention to detail is amazing.  Highly recommend and will definitely stay again.

Sadly a great unit and the team was great to deal with but the advertised accessible access was misleading and incorrect. We arrived with 5 week old baby and someone with mobility issues. The lift not only not working but not there at all. Tammy was apologetic but would have been better if we had been advised before arriving. They did offer a refund if we moved but not practical for a two night stay…hopefully TCH will do the right thing and update their listing to remove details of accessible access. It really impacted our stay.
Well organised and enough ammenities. Water access.
Rude. Rude. Rude. The old woman at front counter didn't have receptionist qualities, and should just clean.
Using the showers is an experience. You have to stand up against the wall in the cubicle to get wet. Not very good.

Great holiday park for a comfy stay in Tweeds. Easy to find. Service is friendly.
Pool with a slide for little or big kids, nice large outdoor bbq area and kids playground. Every night hosts a different food truck for a nice dinner option.
Amenities are really clean 👌
We'd definitely stay again.

Have been going to body's bay caravan park for 22plus years. It is close to awesome gold coast beaches and shopping  centres. Has a camp kitchen, outdoor gym, playground and a pool or swim in the designated  swim area in the river.  Very relaxing.
Toilets and showers weren't very clean. Plus we were meant to be on a site next to my relatives but was separated.  Been coming for years but won't be coming again. Plus the midgees were really bad.

Left earlier than planned. Our first disappointment was when the booking we made for 2 sites was seperated and not able to be sorted.
Also, unfortunately the filthy hygeine habits of patrons was evidently very lacking. When park busy as has been this last week it would be appropriate if the toilets etc were cleaned more than once a day.
Also, being so busy it would of been good if all washing machines were working.
Cannot fault the staff everyone friendly and helpful.  Have stayed here many times and will again but not during holiday time.
Great place!! 👍

We konden hier gelijk terecht met onze campervan. Vriendelijke mensen. Niet erg duidelijk hoe de plekken precies verdeeld zijn, maar er is een groot stuk leeg, dus op een plekje onder de bomen geparkeerd omdat het anders  véél te heet wordt. Na een prachtige wandeling langs het water nu even bijkomen.
Very accomodating, close to water, able to bring boat/jet ski.|Good clean amities|Good communal kitchen.|Alternate food vans on site.|Will Return 🙂
Disgustingly dirty facilities.

We stayed in a Banksia cabin, pay 3 nights, stay for 4. Fantastic value, beautifully presented wee unit. The queen bed was comfy with its own ceiling fan, & inbuilt bunks in the adjoining lounge to accommodate children makes it the perfect spot for a family break away. Our cabin had an outside but adjoining bathroom with a huge double shower & fabulous water pressure. The full kitchen meant we could prepare our own meals with a great range of supermarkets close by.|The onsite facilities are fantastic with a large communal kitchen/ dining space next to the in ground pool. While close to the main road, we weren’t bothered by traffic noise at all, the resident birds were a treat
Staff are so rude….Never again

Beautiful cabin — my children absolutely loved the pool and the games room. The camp kitchen was also great and we were very happy with the stay overall.

However, this changed at 2:30pm on the day of check-out when I received a missed call and text message from the reception staff member, Angela:

“Tweed Heads Holiday Park, Afternoon Danni could you please ring reception please. I just tried to ring your phone and could not leave a message. Just regarding the Cabin that you were in that I need to touch base with you please. Kind regards, Angela.”

I called back, assuming we had accidentally left something behind. Instead, I was informed — in a very blunt and accusatory tone — that one of my children had urinated on the mattress and that I “didn’t have the decency” to let reception know. I was told the cabin “stank of urine” and that the mattress was wet through the protector.

I was then advised that I would be charged a cleaning fee, which would be invoiced directly to my card.
While I fully understand cleaning fees apply in situations like this, the issue was not the fee — it was the way I was spoken to. I was belittled, spoken down to, and treated as though I had intentionally hidden something, when in reality I had absolutely no idea my 4-year-old had wet the bed until after this phone call.

The conversation became so upsetting that I told Angela to just charge my card and to never call me again.

To be clear: I would never ignore or hide something like this if I had known. Accidents happen with young children, and a professional, compassionate approach would have gone a long way.

I also want to acknowledge the receptionist who checked us in on Friday — she was absolutely lovely. If she had been the person to contact me today, I am confident the situation would have been handled with understanding rather than belittling.

Because of how I was treated, I will not be returning, nor will I be recommending this accommodation to others
Wasnt that happy staying here..im big for extra clean bathrooms. These weren't clean. Was a stop over for us heading south. Wouldnt stay again.
Very relaxing cabin was good. It would be nice if they had a carport at every cabin. We will definitely return.
Amenities were spotless! Staff very friendly and helpful.  We have already booked a return trip.

Nice park on the face of it. However, an experience with management seriously soured our experience.
We were one of only two tents in the campground when a ride on mower started up at 7.30am. Fair enough, these things need to be done when they're not busy. However, when the mower was about 40 metres from our tent, something came flying through the air, closely missed my partner and hit the tent, causing a small rip in the fabric. This thing turned out to be a mower-mangled tent peg, which, given the distance and force with which it was thrown, it could have killed someone if it hit them. The mower operator was apologetic, but said we would need to talk to the office. So my partner took the peg to the lady in the office. After seeking advice, she basically accused him of lying, saying they couldn't do anything without proof, and 'how do we know it wasn't already ripped'. (She made sure to take the peg from him and not return it though.) My partner suggested that this could have been nasty if it had hit a person, and she replied 'but it didn't'. And that was that. I'm sure their public liability insurer would be interested to know of this blase attitude to the safety of their guests. Ultimately, the damage to our tent can be patched with a $20 repair kit, and we would have been happy with an apology and a discussion about how this can be prevented in future - ie better protection /guards on the mower - but management proved that they don't really care about guest safety, only money. Having now read some other reviews,  I can see we were not the only ones to come across this attitude.
The park itself is nicely laid out, the facilities are decent,  and the kids loved the pool and slide.  The location is incredibly noisy for campers though, with frequent air, road and water traffic (including night time drag races at nearby intersection) and worst of all , the place is absolutely RIDDLED with sandflies. A heads up from management would have been nice so we could have been sure to paint ourselves liberally with bug repellent before even getting out of the car. It was simply not possible to sit outside the tent and relax. Almost two weeks after leaving we are still itching.
Ultimately, I could not recommend this park to anyone and will never go back.
Family booked Harbour Cabin. 5 star accommodation.  Has everything.

What a lovely park.
Large sites. Clean and tidy.
Stayed 3 nights.
We will definitely use again.  Thankyou to the lovely staff. 👍

Kids have had an amazing time in the pool, the large playground and the small river beach, as well as riding in safety. Other visitors have been very friendly and the staff communication has been excellent, with regular text messages to guests regarding park information. Our cabin was refreshed 4 days into our stay to an incredibly high standard, would definitely return!
Great location with good accessibility. very quiet and peaceful

Arrived on my ETA, reception was closed, waited half an hour before someone inside was able to open, site given to me was taken up by someone else, for a tent site, the ground is rock hard, couldnt get a single tent peg in. Better off camping at the beach and getting the fine, just as much and less worry

Had to leave two days earlier, even though we booked four nights, because the sandflies were everywhere near our camp site and my family including our baby were stung badly all over and are still suffering from the bites which turned blister even four days after. We might be allergic, but I checked with my camp neighbours, and they also showed some bad reactions like swelling. And nobody recevied any heads-up from the provider, otherwise we would've brought some allergy or soothing medications (in fact we brought mosquito repellent, but it didn't work for sandfly, and I don't know what works). The camp neighbour said they probably would never come back again, I think we won't either. The amenities were good though. If you do camp there, do research and be prepared.

We camped here over the King’s Birthday long weekend. The amenities were clean and the location is great, perfect for those who enjoy fishing, with shops and plenty of tourist attractions nearby.

However, a major downside was the sandflies, especially around the camping sites. Despite using insect repellent torches, coils, and spray, I was still bitten hundreds of times and ended up needing medication for an allergic reaction. If you're sensitive to mozzie or sandfly bites, this may not be the ideal spot for you.

Decent location and clean sites. Unfortunately we had to cut our trip short and leave early because despite all our best efforts, we were being completely covered in itchy bites from the midges. They were extremely bad. I know this isn’t something that can be controlled necessarily but because of this, we wouldn’t recommend.

The park overall was lovely and spacious with plenty of trees with great very clean amenities and camp kitchen. We would have loved to use the so called heated pool more but with it being set at 25 it still felt cold. Unfortunately when we spoke to Shane the groundsman he was rude and stated 25 was where it was set and staying. It was a shame as the pool sat empty for the 3 weeks we spent there.
Nice caravan park,  every thing is so clean 👌
Fantastic park. Very clean and tidy. Lovely river views. We had a harbourside cabin. Just perfect. Everything you need. Lovely verandah wirh river view.

Great caravan park,amenities clean and plenty of them,great pool and has water slide, good play ground for children, shops only 5 minutes away by car, very big van sites, can park car beside van, friendly service on arrival

We love this caravan park it’s big, very clean and in a good location close to Broadbeach/surfers paradise, clean amenities. Stayed on the fake grass it’s the best. Close to the wharf where you can buy fresh crabs & prawns. Friendly staff.

Thanks for the stay, we had a great first experience with you. Your park is very clean and well maintained. Staff were great. Also the guy roaming around was very helpful and approachable. Will definately be back.

Close to everything
Heaps of sites
Very clean and neat.
Friend staff
Reasonably priced
I've been coming here for over 35 years
Powered and non powered
Easy caravan access
Pool with a slide - great for kids
Well maintained park. Spacious quiet sites, beautiful beach with great walking path running past. First time stay, but will certainly come back.
Excellent, well run modern caravan park, amenities were always sparkling clean

Wow, one of the best parks we've stayed in this year. Super friendly staff, really well set-out grounds with a central pool and camp kitchen, nice gardens throughout, good sized sites with easy access, four well located amenities buildings (kept spotlessly clean) each with a laundry and ample clothes lines. And the location? It backs onto a beaut little creek with a boat ramp and this leads to the Tweed River, and is right on a cycleway into town (15 minute ride) in one direction, and a great cafe is a 10 minute walk in the other direction. We'll be back for sure.
Best park in Tweed, clean, safe and well organised

Very good holiday park. I've been here many times and have loved it..helpful staff and friendly ..nice clean amenities..spotless..there is a small beach and calm water for kayaking..fishing and chilling out..across the the road is a jetty and beach..beautiful sunrise..bike ride from park to the coolangatta takes 25 minutes..definitely worth it..
It is a good holiday park. Each site is given plenty of room and amenities are OK. Only failure is site Internet, it doesn't work, day or night. Lucky if it stays on 30 seconds..

Service was lovely. Price was very reasonably!! Housed 6 for like $400 for two night, bargain‼️‼️

Location is by the water.

The boom gate with  carregistration recognition was practical. Staff very friendly and helpful. The  Caravan park was very well set up, lovely heated pool with water slide, kitchen and BBQ area looked good. The slabs were pressure cleaned when the sites were empty. The Amenities were large, great water pressure in shower and hot water, blade to clean wet floor in shower,  very strong hand dryer. The basins and toilet bowels could do with more bleach and scrubbing, they were daily cleaned. In ladies Amenities the furthest left basin when turned to hot setting didn't run water, only on cold it had water, the others all worked well. ( did mention this to staff member)
The Park was clean and well maintained, large sites. The cabins looked large and were facing the water, lovely setting. Friendly people all around, nice Caravan park. Would recommend it to everyone.
Excellent location and a well maintained park.
We stayed in a lovely harbour side cabin. Had absolutely everything you might need for a comfortable holiday. Very impressed.

Staff are very friendly and helpful amenities are clean, camp kitchen excellent and all under cover ,locked doors with code to enter, which I really like . Tables I think need to be arranged a little different to how they have them set under cover .Park is very clean and tidy and well maintained. Sites are quite big. We had a site on fake grass and they are just beautiful. I think the Holiday park could do with a coffee van , it’s about 1-2 km away from any coffee shop. There’s a pizza van that comes which is nice.
We felt very safe in the park. Would definitely recommend staying here.

(Nikki Loxton)
Very unimpressed with Tweed Holiday Park Tweed Heads for lack of customer service, compassion and care to their customers/guests.
I booked an unpowered site and called two days before my check-in day as we had had torrential rain all week, and more to come during our stay and all the next week. I understood it was within the two week notice period but explained that weather could not be judged two weeks out, and also that I was in a converted van that was very hard to camp in during wet weather. As we were booked into an unpowered site, I asked if we could just post-pone the stay to two weeks time hoping for better weather, or at least no rain event. The manager did not call me back until the day of check in, at 11am when check-in was at 12pm and I lived three hours away - all to tell me no. And very much a hard no. I wasn’t asking for my money back, I just wanted to post-pone the stay. No reason given, just a hard no.
Unprofessional, unfriendly, unkind, unhelpful - will not be staying nor recommending.
Very disappointing - the manager should be embarrassed.

Great service staff are always friendly.
I âm a mobile auto electrician  repairing caravan so I go into a lot of parks

We have been to this place many times since my first visit in 2008 as a child now i am back with my own! we love the new artificial grass sites and i don’t think we will ever go back to a regular grass site again!
Staff incredible as always, clean amenities, boat ramp and minutes away from local shops! We will be back time and time again!
- Aliesha & family

Wir hatten einem Campingplatz für die Nacht gesucht. Wir hatten nicht gebucht, und hatten großes Glück gehabt, das wir einen schönen Platz bekommen haben. Die Dame an der Rezeption, war sehr freundlich und super nett. Der ganze Platz war sauber und sehr schön. Unser Highlight waren die 100 die da gegen 17;30 Uhr rumgeflogen sind.
Empfehlenswert, für Kinder ideal.

Lovely grassy sites, great pool and amenities BUT the location of the tent sites adjacent to the creek meant we were eaten by biting midges. Not somewhere I’d stay again after wet weather (and there’s been lots of rain the last month or so).

Very clean and tidy park. The swimming pool and camp kitchen are very well kept. They are very strict with their book in times, even though there was no one on our campsite when we arrived. But you must remember the rid. Midges are a nightmare.
Great place to stay. We are return visitors from Perth Western Australia.
Always a beautiful stay
Beautiful place! Reasonably priced, great location, nice facilities

Nice place to stay
Office staff are very friendly grounds guy needs to get some people skills

Excellent place to stay with family. Plenty of options for accommodation so the glamping experts need not rough it with the camling crew. The amenities blocks are clean and well thought out. Hot water is excellent and the water pressure is enviable.
The range of campsites mean you can choose a site that best suits your set up, have a chat with staff if you're unsure of your needs.
The foods vans over the school holidays were excellent and a wonderful idea.
Location is amazing! You are right next to the beautiful Tweed and the onsite boat ramp mean your a short drive from on the water activities. The boat/ car cleaning station is super handy too.
Overall great place to stay.

Great little park , we stayed on a premium site for 2 nights as we passed through tweed . Staff were very accommodating, helpful & friendly . Great size sites , lots of amenities and very clean!! Great pool for the kids with a slide . Creek at the back of the park good for a little fishing , walking distance to the pub across the bridge , awesome little coffee shop just the road , best coffee and breakfast!
Would highly recommend this park
Great location, great pool with a waterslide, good facilities. Also enjoyed floating down the river each day (bring floaties / lilos etc)

This holiday park is really good.

Definitely 5/5

Rates are reasonable and fair, the park is very clean including the toilets, showers and amenities.

Main highlights:
The pool
An awesome river swimming section connected to the park.
Wasn’t over crowded.
Staff are friendly.

The only downside was the Mexican food trailer which guest visits the park, the food was terrible and way overpriced, threw away half of the meals.
Price gouging, to expensive. Result is this place has been almost a ghost town the past six months l have stopped here.
Recently called into to discuss booking options with Jill. Extremely helpful and customer satisfaction focused. A pleasure to chat with her.

Lovely campsite, one of our favourite that we visited on the east coast. Beautiful little beach on the river, and I imagine a great spot if you own a boat too, due to the boat ramp access they have. Facilities were clean and in good condition. We also enjoyed the food van being there in the evening.

Beautiful clean park.
Large grass sites.
New clean amenities.
Nice playground for kids and Beautiful pool and slide.
Very quite and well maintained.

Last time. I was there  l wanted to go to Brisbane early.  The boom is in a little gate would not open for me to drive out  my l rang the after hours number spoke to a girl .she said she would  contact them . Nothing . I gave up.sfter an hr . They. I went and got it re keyed when the office opened. The lady proceeded to advise me of the 5 things l probably did for this to happen .  Like follow some one in ect.  Then gave me the old smug smile when l said l had done none of these things. The system is. And out dated  it should be replaced. And for what they are charging for a small strip of grass ,they could replace the system now .

I went there with my family for vacation and had an amazing time, we slept in a tent but you could also bring your caravan or book rooms. There was a river for boating and kayaking nearby. The toilets were clean, there was a mini kitchen with an electric stove and refrigerator.
Great place, very recommended

No fault of the management, avoid staying on the side of the mangroves, at all cost. We had spray on and still they had a feast on us. Park was clean and well maintained, just be aware when booking sites. Stay towards the ocean/highway side.
Excellent holiday park. Great cabins and swimming pool.

Staying at Boyds Bay Holiday Park in the caravan.
A nice tidy park with clean facilities, large pool, lovely camp kitchen.  Nice shady trees with the gardens well maintained.  Lots of different birds.  Located on the water, bay not surf.

This park is very central to a lot of places like the Southern Gold Coast, Kingscliff, Fingal, Brunswick Heads and Ballina.

It can be a bit noisy, eg traffic and not far from airport but we got used to it.

Very friendly staff & helpful.Huge park & on the Tweed River with lovely views.It has 4 amenities blocks which are spotlessly cleaned & good size.The sites are good & vary in size,there are slabs & artificial grass ones.The  Cabins  are lovely & will hold any number of guests.Grounds are kept lovely.Beautiful heated pool & very good playground.Close to shopping centres & to tourist attractions.
Everything to keep the family busy.
I wasn't staying there, my brother and sister in law were. They love the place and on the times I have visited them, I found the place very clean and friendly.
Great place for a holiday. Good swimming pool with waterslide for the kids. Cabins were a good size and had all the things you need in the kitchen. Good BBQ facilities around the park.

OVERRAN WITH A MOUSE INFESTATION
if staying here take mouse bait/traps

unfortunately both times we have stayed here we have had to repair something afterwards. if staying take precautions. first time a mouse chewed a whole in our annex second time a mouse got into our van resulting in everything needing to be taken out and sanitised.

staff are unfriendly and not helpful

Great short stay good location right on the river fantastic clean amenities and you can fish and the fish can straight on the bbq. That have heaps of sites and the park can accommodate all sizes of vans
Double cabin was filled with insects, no screen door (did not help) & shower curtain was mouldy. Lovely location, just needs more attention to detail & maintenance.

I rarely give reviews but this place is worthy of it!
We stayed here on a powered site for 3 nights. Lovely green, well maintained park with friendly staff. Very clean amenities with key pad entry.. felt safe with the kids. Great heated pool too. Washing facilities were excellent… tap to pay machines 👏🏻 I’m so over the old coin ones.. that are surprisingly still at most holiday parks.
This is up there with the best campsites we’ve stayed at on the East Coast! And we’ve been to a few… from Melb to Cape York and back. 😊

We would have given a higher review if we weren't abused by reception when leaving. A miscommunication between co-pilot and driver to wait before opening the boom gate resulted in it closing before we exited. We were abused by the reception lady. Whilst I acknowledge we did the wrong thing there was no need to yell at us for an innocent park.

Park was lovely with great facilities, nice pool and was cool to have a pizza truck on site the night we stayed. However we won't be returning again after being abused.

The pizza van AVOID at all costs the prices on everything is an absolute joke for what you get $9 for a burnt garlic bread and very thin pizza with TIN MUSHROOMS on it I find it pretty unfair for the price very very cheaply made makes dominos look fancy. Domino’s is down the road you will get so much more and  better quality and at a cheaper price disgusting 🤮
We had a fake grassed site good. Amenities clean. Good pool & bbq area

First visit to the Park. Had a lovely cabin. Roomy, and very clean and comfortable. Great location on the river. One of the best cabins we've stayed in.  Regular communications from management via text - even to tell us "the pizza van will be there tonight from 5-7!!!"
Well done - we'll be back!!!
Nice Caravan park with all the amenities.
We love staying here for weekend trips to Cooloongatta 😀❤️
Great location.Had a problem with site and all was sorted out.Will definitely go back

Stayed on our caravan for 5 nights. Great spot just off main street. Has boat ramp to river.
Didn't stay in cabins but from outside they looked very nice.
Staff were friendly and assisted with any enquiry.
Nice little coffee shop  about 5 minute walk out and to right, with main shops back on main road either left or right at lights.
Happy to stay here again. Good size camp kitchen, wood fired pizzas available on Wednesday nights which was delicious.
Nice little place to go camping u can go fishing swimming in a pool they even have a water slide just be careful how fast u go bc it has some edgy corners but ye over all it’s a nice little place 👍
One of the best Caravan parks I have stayed in! First class facilities, immaculately presented and clean! Lovely management beautiful location. I highly recommend this park! Thank you 🙏
Staying on one of the newer powered sites with the artificial grass it’s fantastic.

I would have to say one of the best designed caravan parks I have been to.
The swimming pool heated to 28 degrees.
Pizza truck nights were good, pizzas nice.
Amenities, lots of them, clean, nice, and hot, 2 dump points.
Caravan sites well sized, discuss with them if you require a site larger.
Make sure you have a ruler to connect your gate tag with electrical tape so you can easily reach the boom gate or ask management.

Wow, well done, this is a TOP park.

Bus stop to Tweedheads just out the front, they have there own boat launch area.
Very friendly staff great service and and very nice and clean place to stay
Great campsite, nice little lake and close to river front. We paid around $42 for a non electrical spot
Always super clean, staff always great, love the location as I can walk everywhere!
Warning for families with kids, druggies are staying on the camp site not a safe place.

Full of midgies /sandflies. Booked for a week with our group BUT it was impossible to stay. We asked for assistance in booking another campsite within their group of tweed council parks but were given  very blunt and aggressive NO. Also a giant No to any refund.

I stayed here last week. I was very disappointed with how the amenities were presented and serviced.
There was no toilet paper in the women’s toilets.
It smelt like stale urine. And the showers looked like they have not been scrubbed in months.
It was a very unpleasant experience.
I was unable to bath my baby in the baby bath due to the horrendous uncleanliness.
Management needs to take more note on who they are hiring when it comes to their staff and cleaners.

Wouldn’t be on my list of parks to recommend.
Used sanitary items on the grass opposite our site for 3 days until a lawn mower rode over them.
Urinals look and smell like the have never been throughly cleaned.
Games room smelt like urine.
Homeless people living in tiny broken tents wasn’t very appealing either.
The water slide is broken for approximately 12months and there’s only maybe 6 pool chairs 🫤
Much better parks around where you don’t get eaten alive by mozzies and midges to.

This would have to be one of the most well kept and thought out caravan parks we have ever been to. Great value for money option so close to everything.
The facilities are so clean and tidy as are the sites and grounds around the park.
The sites are fairly spacious for the average van and car and the ensuite bathroom sites are super lux for a little spoiling.
There is a beautiful pool and playground with only a 5 minute drive to the beautiful beaches over the border in cooloongatta and a 2 minute drive to Tweed city shopping centre for all that you could require.
We'll definitely be back here again.

Nicely located park, with direct access to water (creek).

The gate access was not user friendly.
You needed to hang hlf body outside of your window to reach the gate beeper.

Not ideal and rather annoying. Easy fix if the place provides beeper with longer handles.

The midges were ruthless!!!

We were literally eaten alive. So if you have allergies on insect bites, you might not want to come here.

Whilst this is a great park and I have stayed here on a few occasions there are a couple of issues I would like to raise-||On both visits I got eaten alive by Midges and so did numerous other visitors we |connected with, surely the management can find some form of spray to avoid visitors|running around scratching themselves for their entire visit or maybe warn visitors on entry that the park is infested with Midges.||Also the Amenities block (west) obviously had a malfunctioning urinal flushing issue as it reeked of stale urine which made the use of the amenity  very unpleasant.  I would have thought the cleaning staff would have reported this to management but it was there the whole time we were there (10 days) and could be clearly smelt from outside the amenities block.
Fantastic location.

Sensational service and location, great play area , swimming pool and value for money. The artificial grass sites are super good.  The park also has a boat ramp and excellent swimming and fishing grounds.
What a lovely park. Great pool. Amenities block very clean and lovely surroundings.

Thoroughly enjoyed our trip. Good spot with a large playground and pool, perfect for kids.

Handy location to most things, and having a private boat ramp was excellent.

Have rebooked for next year.
Too busy, small sites, kids on scooters and bikes all over the place where not allowed.
Always great staff to help from reception to the guy on the wipper snipper great weekend there thanks
Great park, first time there. Central to shops and beaches. Sites were a decent size. Amenities clean. Will return.
Great weekend with our van on an enquire site. Next to best River for kayaking
Fantastic stay Kim and the team are happy to oblige booked Xmas 2021.

A great holiday park. We had a powered site. Amenities were very good and clean, the camp kitchen were excellent. Very quiet in the area we were in. Pool was definitely not heated! The only downside that may prevent us going back, was the abundance of midges, that made being outside difficult. Of course, not the fault of the park, but something to be aware of if you retract badly to insect bites, as we did.

Not worth it, they messed up my booking and when I asked for a refund they were unpolite and unwilling to do anything about it. Also there is a massive waterway pollution going on with their car wash station, sending the soap water straight to the creek. Save yourself the hassle and book elsewhere.

We stayed on a van site - sites weren’t big but we could mostly fit what we needed.  Park was very tidy and clean.  Amenities were always clean.  Great central location. Walking distance to grocery store. Located near the water with a boat ramp. The pool was excellent and loved that it had a playground and kids room to keep the smaller kids occupied.

The park and surrounds were very well kept and the cabin we stayed in was great, clean and tidy, great spot, unfortunately the same can not be said for the staff, after having problems with the lousy  boom gate we were spoken to pretty rudely and accused of doing things to it to make it not work (none of which we had done). maybe try be a bit more helpful when people are paying so much to stay in places like this now.
Great location short distance from everything

Customer service was not great from managers. Both husband and wife were rude, opinionated, I was not advised about the 1 car policy per site or cabin, my son and his wife had a car each in the park whilst staying in a cabin and the office blocked their gate fob. It wasn't till I had to drop my other son to work that I encounter a very disturbing situation with the male manager. I then bought it up with his wife and she too got verbally aggressive and defensive when I advised of the situation earlier that morning with her husband.
Better communication is needed on check in. Wasn't a great experience. Definitely left a bad taste in the mouth, won't be back in a hurry.

Please be aware that this park has roads adjacent to its boundaries that create significant road noise throughout the day and in particular at night.
In addition they have a resident crow that literally squarks all hours of the night making it difficult to get a good night sleep.
We are just wanting to make you aware of what we experienced during our stay.
Very welcoming and clean park
Well presented grounds, immaculate bathrooms, big sites, nice heated pool, direct access to Tweed River with concrete boat ramp. Safe little swimming area and beach
Friendly park ,, great location  . 👍.

Pro: Unit was tidy and nicely furnished. The amenities were nice and clean. The staff were friendly and helpful. The park is well set out and the location is beautiful.
Con: The couches and cushions were a bit grubby, the coffee machine was broken in ours and our neighbours unit. The wifi didn’t work, not that was necessarily a bad thing for the kids but didn’t help up with work and study commitments. Access to the park with two cars was a pain. When you have cabins that cater for a family of six, you should probably consider the possibility of multiple cars. I understand the one car parking rule, but unloading and loading cars on arrival and departure was troublesome.

Apart from the above mentioned issues we had a great time, we would definitely recommend the park to friends and will return in the future. Overall its a 4 out of 5 from us.
Plenty of powered sites. Amenities always clean. Lovely pool with water slide.
Immaculately maintained, generous sites, easy to park, hard to get a vacancy but worth the wait!
Great caravan park close to major shopping centres & pubs / restaurants. Clean & well aired amenities. Friendly& helpful staff.

A lovely holiday park for caravans. Cabins look very nice. Great resort pool and camp kitchen. Grounds are well maintained although slabs are not very big. 4 large amenities blocks that are kept clean. Staff helpful. No pets.

The best caravan park I've ever stayed in, bar none. Fantastic location, great facilities, helpful staff, awesome landscaping, just all positives.|Have endured substandard caravan parks from Gold Coast to Cooktown, nothing comes even close to th experience here at Twee Heads. Have booked in for a fortnight, planning to stay much longer.

Magnifique , accueil chaleureux personnel super sympas belle endroit pour y aller en couple , seul , en famille . Bien située et bien entretenu.
Douche et toilette super propre .
Merci .
A great location close to everything and a quite friendly park.
Great place to stay, right on the water close to shopping centre & club's.
Great value and location

Great camping spot. Splashed out for a powered ensuite site. Being able to leave personal belongings in shower felt just like a hotel room.  Pricing was cheaper than most parks we have stayed at recently.
Definitely on our return list.
Great powered sites
